title: Truth of Facts Recited in Written Instrument
text: The facts recited in a written instrument are conclusively presumed to be true as between the parties thereto, or their successors in interest; but this rule does not apply to the recital of a consideration.

structure.division_title: Burden of Proof; Burden of Producing Evidence; Presumptions and Inferences

notes: Creates a conclusive presumption that factual recitals in a written instrument are true between the parties or their successors in interest, except as to recital of consideration.
